select_when: The first version is unlikely to be correct, so the cheapest path to quality is repeated prototype-test-learn cycles.
Uncertainty can be reduced faster through bounded learning loops than through one long round of upfront planning.
A team needs a way to keep revising a hypothesis, design, or solution path as new evidence accumulates.
The work is drifting toward static planning even though progress depends on comparing each version against the last one.
avoid_when: The team is iterating without stop rules, success thresholds, or a decision about what evidence is strong enough to commit.
Local polishing is continuing even though the underlying problem framing or hypothesis likely needs a reset.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on bounded refinement cycles, hypothesis revision, and stop-rule discipline rather than on broad innovation-process language.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor process sprawl because the raw source is already strong on prototype-test-learn loops and the one-day-answer discipline.
Did not add higher-order composition fields because this batch stops at the frozen Wave 1 to Wave 3 contracts.
open_questions: The output type compresses a source that spans design loops, project synthesis, and skill acquisition.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit that iteration is strongest when uncertainty falls through repeated bounded cycles rather than one-shot planning.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions come directly from the source danger lines on endless looping and local polishing without reframing.

input_type.note: The raw source activates on refinement and hypothesis-testing loops, but the compact input label is reviewed operational normalization.

output_type.note: The source yields reusable cycle structures and learning loops; the output label compresses that into a typed artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Iteration clearly uses evidence-updating and reflection, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
